narrative_scene_description: On the left side of the street stands the State Bank of India (SBI) Baghbazar Branch, characterized by a multi-story facade and blue-and-white painted curbs. Opposite the SBI branch is an Amul outlet and the 'GHOSH & SON'S' shop, where several people are sitting or standing near small stalls. To the right of the Amul outlet is a narrow side street that branches off between older urban buildings. Adjacent to this alleyway is the Sterling Hospital zone, marked by green bushes, a 'NO PARKING BOTH SIDE' circular sign, and a 'No Entry' traffic sign. Beside the hospital signage is a shop named 'Saphire'. To the right of Saphire stands a green building decorated with Brazil flag paintings on its ground floor. Adjacent to this decorated building is another Sterling Hospital sign and a yellow-walled shop named 'RADHEY' with closed shutters. To the left of the hospital sign is a small shack-like structure featuring a green 'Fairars Caterer' signboard. Beside this structure stands a large green directional sign board indicating routes to Shyam Bazar, Dunlop, and Lockgate Road. Opposite this sign is a traffic island containing the 'DOYEN' monument, which features a bronze statue of a standing figure on a pedestal. To the right of the Doyen monument is a long boundary wall covered in colorful murals and posters, including those for 'ROOT' and 'Satyameva Jayate'. At the end of this wall is a large red billboard with the text 'Parimal' in Bengali. Beside the Parimal billboard is an older multi-story building housing a 'MENTAL HEALTH COUNSELLING' center and another Amul sign. Positioned near the base of this building is a 'GO SLOW' regulatory traffic sign.
temporal_progression_description: At the beginning of the clip at 00:01, the State Bank of India (SBI) Baghbazar Branch appears on the left side of the road. Shortly after at 00:02, the Amul and Ghosh & Son's shops become visible near a narrow intersection where people are gathered under a large tree. As the vehicle continues forward around 00:03 to 00:05, several Sterling Hospital signs, a 'No Parking' sign, and a green building with Brazil flags come into view. Near 00:06, the 'RADHEY' shop appears. At 00:09, a porter carrying a heavy sack on his shoulder is seen on the sidewalk. From 00:10 to 00:19, a large white truck with a blue tarp and diamond-patterned decorations dominates the foreground as it passes the ego-vehicle. Further along the road at 00:22, a green shack for 'Fairars Caterer' and a large green directional sign board for Shyam Bazar and Dunlop appear. At 00:24, the vehicle reaches a central intersection featuring the 'DOYEN' statue monument. As the vehicle continues between 00:26 and 00:28, it passes a long boundary wall covered in various political and social posters, including a 'ROOT' sign. Near 00:29, a large red 'Parimal' billboard appears on a street corner. Near the end of the clip at 00:30, an older building with signs for 'Mental Health Counselling' and an 'Amul' outlet is seen, followed by a 'GO SLOW' traffic instruction sign.
